Typecheck clean, lint clean (after one strict-boolean-expressions fix in PatrolSelector following the user-dialogs commit 29e66b0 pattern), 32/32 web router tests pass.

              SONNET THRASHING — REPRODUCED (session memory confirms):
              Original dispatch of this item to a Sonnet 4.6 subagent (estimated 22K) thrashed (autocompact 3× in 3 turns) and was stopped per memory-governance.md §4. The subagent had created the branch and partial files before thrashing — Opus took over the remaining work directly. This confirms `feedback_context_thrashing.md`: Sonnet effective workspace ≤15K in this project, NOT the 30K theoretical §1 Step 2.5 budget, due to Vercel hook noise (next-forge, shadcn, react-best-practices skill injections per Read) + per-prompt directive accumulation. Going forward: split aggressively before Sonnet dispatch, or execute directly in Opus when total work is small.

NEXT:         Phase 8 Batch 2 Item 2 — map patrol-area polygon overlays. Tier 2 (~8-12K).
              Wire `trpc.map.patrolAreas.list` (already exists, returns `polygonGeojson` + `colorHex` per area) through imperative MapLibre `addSource({ type: "geojson", data: polygonGeojson })` + `addLayer({ type: "fill", paint: { "fill-color": colorHex, "fill-opacity": 0.25 } })` calls via the forwarded `MapRef` from the mapcn primitive. mapcn does NOT expose a polygon component, so extract a small `MapPolygon` client component to encapsulate the imperative dance + useEffect cleanup. Closes the map feature group.

              Opus-direct recommended (do NOT dispatch to Sonnet at this scope — thrashing risk confirmed twice in this project).
